commit_scope: Rebase the Telegram cohesion refactor onto current main and align the maintainability baseline with observed rebased metrics.

commands: cd api && python3 -m pytest -q tests/test_monitor_pipeline_stale_running.py tests/test_agent_control_plane.py tests/test_agent_integration_api.py tests/test_agent_execute_endpoint.py tests/test_agent_pipeline.py
cd api && python3 -m ruff check app/routers/agent_telegram.py app/routers/agent_telegram_format.py app/routers/agent_tasks_routes.py app/routers/agent_run_state_routes.py
local_validation.summary: The rebase kept the Telegram formatting extraction intact, regenerated the router/root indexes, and corrected the maintainability baseline to the actual rebased audit metrics: layer violations 3, large modules 44, very large modules 17, long functions 112, placeholders 9, risk score 996. This remains an improvement over current main before the PR: large modules 45, long functions 113, risk score 1006. Focused agent tests, ruff, index check, whitespace check, wellness, and the maintainability audit pass.

ci_validation.notes: Pending force-push of the rebased PR branch and GitHub checks on PR #2537.

deploy_validation.notes: No deploy attempted for this rebase repair yet. Public deployment is currently aligned to main commit 4eb7e73f before PR #2537 lands.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: Telegram webhook behavior remains routed through the same endpoint while formatting helpers live in agent_telegram_format.py. Task alert importers now point at the helper module directly.
test_flows: Ruff passes on the extracted Telegram router/helper and importer files.
Maintainability audit reports regression=no against the rebased baseline.

phase_gate.reason: The rebase repair is locally proven; remote CI, merge, deploy, and public verification remain pending.
